author: y4rr
content:
Plan: Phase 3 (#996) was scoped strictly to PERMUTE-pattern CPU-pull bugs. Five additional CPU-pull sites in ViT and Swin remain after Phase 3, each calling for a different ferrotorch-core primitive than permute:

ViT (vit.rs):
- 202-246: batched MLP unrolling — slices each batch element [B,seq_len,embed_dim]→[seq_len,embed_dim], runs MLP, concatenates back. Wants tensor-level broadcasting Linear or batched matmul that accepts 3-D input.
- 381-421 / 534-549: cls_token concat (1 leading-token prepend along dim 1). Wants ferrotorch-core ::cat(dim) or torch-shaped torch.cat.
- 405-422 / 552-566: pos_embed add with broadcast across batch. Wants broadcast addition or arithmetic::add to accept [B,S,E] + [1,S,E].
- 433-447 / 580-592: cls slice (narrow dim=1 length=1). Wants ::narrow / ::select / ::index_select.

Swin (swin.rs):
- 110-136: batched MLP unrolling — same as ViT. Wants 3-D Linear / batched matmul.
- 422-456 / 571-602: manual mean reduction over the token dim (sum-then-divide). Wants ::mean(dim=1, keepdim=false).

These are out-of-Phase-3 scope. Phase 3 scope was strictly permute-pattern migration as per the architect's pre-flight. Filed here as a Phase 4 candidate so they don't fall through.

Out of scope for #996. Acceptance: each of the 5 sites above replaced with the matching primitive once it lands in ferrotorch-core; ViT and Swin forward stays on input.device() end-to-end with no .data_vec()/TensorStorage::cpu/.to(device) round-trip in production code.
